notes: NT 4.0 included SMP enhancements; evolved into Windows 2000 Server then Windows Server 2003+
Pentium Pro 200MHz in Compaq ProLiant 5000; Klamath expected at 266MHz; Deschutes at 333MHz
Alpha running at 500 MHz in 1996; AlphaServer 5/35 at 32 processors; line discontinued when HP absorbed DEC
OLTP benchmark; tpmC (transactions per minute C); used to segment server market by performance tier
Scalability approach for 16-32+ processor NT systems; Sequent NUMACentre achieved 48,793 tpmC by 1998
NT cluster standard released 1997 as MSCS (Microsoft Cluster Server); evolved into Windows Server Failover Clustering
Set TPC-C price/performance record at $65/tpmC Sept 1996; evolved through SQL Server 7, 2000, etc.
